terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.description: These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern your use of MatchPulse, a hockey tournament management and live scoring platform accessible through our website
terms.html:
These Terms and Conditions ("Terms") govern your use of MatchPulse, a hockey tournament management and live scoring platform accessible through our website and web application (the "Platform"). MatchPulse is operated by the entity referred to in these Terms as "MatchPulse", "we", "us" or "our".

By creating an account, purchasing a plan, or otherwise using the Platform, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.

1. Definitions

Organiser means a person or organisation that creates or administers a competition, tournament, league or fixture on the Platform.

Player means an individual whose participation, profile or statistics are recorded on the Platform.

Manager means a person who administers a team or a group of Players.

Parent means a parent or legal guardian of a Player who is a minor.

Spectator means a person who uses the Platform to view competitions, results or statistics.

User means any person who uses the Platform, including Organisers, Players, Managers, Parents and Spectators.

Paid Plan means any paid plan we offer, as described on our pricing page. Some Paid Plans are once-off and some are recurring.

Content means any data, text, results, scores, images, team information, player information and other material submitted to or generated on the Platform.

2. Accounts

2.1. You may need an account to use certain features. You are responsible for the accuracy of the information you provide and for keeping your login details secure.

2.2. You are responsible for all activity that happens under your account. Tell us promptly if you believe your account has been accessed without your permission.

2.3. You must be old enough to enter into a binding agreement under South African law to hold an account in your own name. If you are a minor, a Parent must consent to and manage your account, as set out in clause 6.

2.4. We may suspend or close an account that breaches these Terms, our Acceptable Use Policy, or applicable law.

3. Roles and responsibilities

3.1. Organisers are responsible for the competitions they run on the Platform, including fixtures, results, and the accuracy of the information they publish. Organisers confirm the outcome of every match through the Platform's manual confirmation process.

3.2. Organisers cannot create Player profiles. Only a Player, a Parent, or a Manager may create and manage a Player profile. Organisers — and the team staff or scorers they authorise — may add an existing Player to a squad and to a match line-up, and may record that Player's results and statistics; what they cannot do is create the Player's profile. This is a deliberate design choice, and it means the person who creates a profile is responsible for that profile and for any consent required to publish it.

3.3. Players, Parents and Managers who create profiles are responsible for the information in those profiles and confirm they are entitled to publish it. Where a profile relates to a minor, clause 6 applies.

3.4. Spectators may view public competition information subject to these Terms.

4. Content and ownership

4.1. You keep ownership of the Content you submit. By submitting Content, you grant us a licence to host, display, process and distribute it for the purpose of operating the Platform, including showing competition results and statistics on public pages.

4.2. You are responsible for the Content you submit and confirm you have the right to submit it.

4.3. We may remove Content that breaches these Terms, our Acceptable Use Policy, or the law, or that we are required to remove following a valid request under clause 6 or our Privacy Policy.

4.4. Competition results, standings and statistics generated by the Platform's stats engine are provided for general information. We take reasonable care to calculate them correctly, but Organisers are responsible for confirming match outcomes, and we do not warrant that every figure is free of error.

5. Plans, pricing and payment

5.1. We offer a free plan and one or more Paid Plans. The plans available at any time, what each one includes, and their prices are set out on our pricing page. Prices are in South African Rand and include VAT where applicable.

5.2. We may change our plans and pricing. Any change applies to new purchases and to renewals that fall due after the change takes effect. The pricing page always reflects the current position.

5.3. Payments are processed by PayFast. By purchasing a Paid Plan, you also agree to PayFast's terms. We do not store your full card details.

5.4. The pricing principle is simple: you pay for what you host, not for what you play in. Participating in a competition someone else hosts does not require a Paid Plan.

Renewals

5.5. Some Paid Plans are once-off, and some renew automatically for a further period unless you cancel before the renewal date. Where a plan renews automatically, we will send you a reminder by email before each renewal so you can decide whether to continue. Whether a plan is once-off or recurring is shown at the time of purchase and on our pricing page.

5.6. You can cancel automatic renewal at any time before the renewal date. Cancellation stops the next charge; it does not end your current paid period early.

Refunds

5.7. You may request a refund of a Paid Plan before a competition has begun or holds active data under that plan. Once a competition tied to the plan has started or contains active match data, the plan is treated as used and no refund is payable. This applies to both once-off and recurring plans.

5.8. Clause 5.7 sets out your rights under this policy. It does not limit any right you have under the Consumer Protection Act or the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act that cannot be excluded by agreement.

6. Minors

6.1. The Platform may record the names and statistics of Players who are minors, for example in schoolboy or junior hockey.

6.2. A Player profile relating to a minor may only be created and managed by the Player's Parent, or by a Manager acting with the Parent's consent. Whoever creates the profile confirms that they have the authority and any consent required to publish the minor's information.

6.3. A Parent may ask us to correct or remove a minor's information at any time. Requests are handled as described in our Privacy Policy.

7. Acceptable use

Your use of the Platform is subject to our Acceptable Use Policy, which forms part of these Terms.

8. Availability and changes

8.1. We aim to keep the Platform available and working well, but we do not guarantee uninterrupted access. We may carry out maintenance, updates and changes, and we may add, change or remove features.

8.2. We may change these Terms. If we make a material change, we will take reasonable steps to let you know. Continuing to use the Platform after a change means you accept the updated Terms.

9. Liability

9.1. To the extent permitted by law, the Platform is provided "as is". We do not give warranties beyond those we cannot lawfully exclude.

9.2. To the extent permitted by law, we are not liable for indirect or consequential loss, or for loss arising from Content submitted by Users, from decisions made based on results or statistics, or from matters outside our reasonable control.

9.3. Nothing in these Terms limits liability that cannot be limited by law, including liability for death or personal injury caused by our negligence, or for fraud.

10. Suspension and termination

10.1. You may stop using the Platform at any time. Where you hold a Paid Plan, clause 5 governs renewals and refunds.

10.2. We may suspend or end your access if you breach these Terms, our Acceptable Use Policy, or the law, or where we are required to by law.

11. General

11.1. These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of South Africa, and the South African courts have jurisdiction.

11.2. If any part of these Terms is found to be unenforceable, the rest remains in force.

11.3. These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy and Acceptable Use Policy, are the whole agreement between you and us regarding the Platform.

12. Contact

For any question about these Terms, contact us using our contact form.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: This Privacy Policy explains how MatchPulse ("we", "us", "our") collects and uses personal information when you use our hockey tournament management and li
privacy.html:
This Privacy Policy explains how MatchPulse ("we", "us", "our") collects and uses personal information when you use our hockey tournament management and live scoring platform (the "Platform"). It is written to meet our obligations under the Protection of Personal Information Act, 2013 (POPIA).

For the purposes of POPIA, MatchPulse is the responsible party for the personal information described below. Our contact details are at the end of this policy.

1. What we mean by personal information

Personal information is information that identifies or relates to a living person. On the Platform, this can include a person's name, the team they play for, their age group, and their playing statistics. A name on its own is personal information, and so is a name combined with playing data. We treat it accordingly.

2. Information we collect

Account information. When you create an account, we collect information such as your name, email address, and login details. Different roles (Organiser, Player, Manager, Parent, Spectator) may provide different information.

Player profile information. When a Player, Parent or Manager creates a Player profile, we collect the information they choose to include, such as the Player's name, team, age group and statistics.

Competition information. When Organisers run competitions, we process fixtures, results, scores and standings, which may include Player names and statistics.

Payment information. When you buy a Paid Plan, payment is handled by PayFast. We receive confirmation of payment and related transaction details. We do not store your full card details.

Technical information. We collect information about how you access the Platform, such as device and browser information and log data, and information collected through cookies. See our Cookie Policy for detail.

3. Who creates Player profiles, and why it matters

Organisations and Organisers cannot create Player profiles. Only a Player, a Parent, or a Manager can create and manage a Player profile. Organisers, and the team staff or scorers they authorise, may add an existing Player to a squad or a match line-up and record their statistics — but they cannot create the Player's profile.

This means the person who creates a profile is responsible for the information in it and for any consent required to publish it. Where a profile relates to a minor, the Parent (or a Manager acting with the Parent's consent) is responsible for that consent. We explain how to correct or remove information in clause 8.

4. How we use personal information

We use personal information to:

  • provide and operate the Platform, including running competitions and displaying results and statistics;
  • create and manage accounts and Player profiles;
  • calculate standings and statistics;
  • process payments for Paid Plans;
  • send service messages, including renewal reminders for recurring plans;
  • keep the Platform secure and prevent misuse;
  • comply with our legal obligations.

We rely on the lawful bases in POPIA for this processing, including performance of our agreement with you, our legitimate interests in operating the Platform, your consent where it is required, and compliance with the law.

5. Public pages

Competition results, standings and Player statistics are shown on public pages, and these pages may be indexed by search engines. This means Player names and statistics can be publicly visible. Anyone creating a Player profile should understand this before publishing information about a Player, particularly a minor.

6. Children's information

The Platform may hold personal information about minors, for example in junior hockey.

Under POPIA, processing a minor's personal information generally requires the consent of a competent person, usually a parent or guardian. Because only a Player, Parent or Manager can create a Player profile, the responsibility for obtaining that consent rests with the person who creates the profile.

A Parent may ask us to correct or remove a minor's information at any time. We will act on a valid request as set out in clause 8.

7. Sharing personal information

We do not sell personal information. We share it only where needed to run the Platform:

  • Service providers who help us operate, such as our hosting provider (Google Firebase), our payment processor (PayFast), and our email delivery provider. These providers process information on our behalf and under agreement.
  • Publicly, to the extent competition results and statistics appear on public pages as described in clause 5.
  • Where required by law, or to protect our rights, users or the public.

Some providers may process information outside South Africa. Where they do, we take reasonable steps to ensure the information is protected in line with POPIA.

8. Your rights

Under POPIA you have the right to:

  • ask what personal information we hold about you;
  • ask us to correct information that is wrong or out of date;
  • ask us to delete information, subject to our legal obligations;
  • object to certain processing;
  • withdraw consent where we rely on it;
  • complain to the Information Regulator.

To exercise any of these, or to ask us to correct or remove a Player profile, contact us using the details below. We may need to confirm your identity, or the identity and authority of a Parent, before we act. We aim to respond within a reasonable time.

If a Player profile was created by someone else, we may need to contact that person as part of handling your request, but you can always come to us directly.

9. How long we keep information

We keep personal information for as long as needed to run the Platform and to keep an accurate record of competitions, and for as long as the law requires. Historic competition results may be retained as part of the sporting record. Where information is no longer needed, we delete or de-identify it.

10. Security

We take reasonable technical and organisational steps to protect personal information, including access controls and secure hosting. No system is completely secure, but we work to keep information safe and to respond properly if something goes wrong.

11. Changes to this policy

We may update this policy from time to time. If we make a material change, we will take reasonable steps to let you know. The "last updated" date at the top shows when it last changed.

12. Contact and complaints

For any privacy question or request, contact our Information Officer using our contact form.

You also have the right to complain to the regulator:

Information Regulator (South Africa) Website: inforegulator.org.za Email: [insert current Information Regulator complaints email]

cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.description: This Cookie Policy explains how MatchPulse ("we", "us", "our") uses cookies and similar technologies on our platform (the "Platform"). It should be read al
cookies.html:
This Cookie Policy explains how MatchPulse ("we", "us", "our") uses cookies and similar technologies on our platform (the "Platform"). It should be read alongside our Privacy Policy.

1. What cookies are

Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. Similar technologies, such as local storage, work in comparable ways. They help a site remember your actions and preferences, keep you signed in, and understand how the site is used.

2. How we use them

We use cookies and similar technologies to:

Keep the Platform working. These are necessary for core features, such as signing you in, keeping you signed in, and remembering your session. The Platform does not work properly without them.

Remember your preferences. These remember choices you make, so you don't have to set them each time.

Understand usage. These help us see how the Platform is used so we can improve it. This information is used in aggregate wherever possible.

We do not use cookies to sell your information.

3. Third-party cookies

Some cookies are set by the services we use to run the Platform, such as our hosting provider (Google Firebase) and our payment processor (PayFast) during checkout. These providers set cookies to deliver their part of the service. Their use of cookies is governed by their own policies.

4. Managing cookies

You can control cookies through your browser settings, including blocking or deleting them. If you block cookies that are necessary for the Platform to work, some features may not function properly, including signing in.

Most browsers let you:

  • see what cookies are stored and delete them;
  • block cookies from specific sites or all sites;
  • clear cookies when you close the browser.

Check your browser's help pages for how to do this.

5. Changes to this policy

We may update this policy as our use of cookies changes. The "last updated" date shows when it last changed.

6. Contact

For any question about this policy, contact us using our contact form.

acceptable-use.title: Acceptable Use Policy
acceptable-use.description: This Acceptable Use Policy sets out the rules for using MatchPulse (the "Platform"). It forms part of our Terms and Conditions. By using the Platform, you
acceptable-use.html:
This Acceptable Use Policy sets out the rules for using MatchPulse (the "Platform"). It forms part of our Terms and Conditions. By using the Platform, you agree to follow it.

The aim is simple: keep the Platform accurate, fair, and safe for everyone, especially for the young players whose names and statistics may appear on it.

1. General conduct

You agree to use the Platform lawfully and honestly. You must not use it in a way that harms others, misrepresents results, or interferes with how it works.

2. Accurate competition data

Organisers and Managers must record fixtures, results and statistics honestly. Do not enter false results, manipulate standings, or misrepresent what happened in a match.

3. Player profiles and other people's information

You must not:

  • create a Player profile for someone you are not entitled to represent;
  • publish a minor's information without the consent of a parent or guardian;
  • add information about another person that is false, misleading, or that you have no right to publish.

Only a Player, Parent or Manager may create and manage a Player profile. If you create a profile, you are responsible for it.

4. Prohibited content

Do not upload, publish or share content that is:

  • unlawful, defamatory, harassing, threatening or abusive;
  • discriminatory or hateful;
  • obscene, or otherwise inappropriate in the context of a sporting platform used by young players;
  • infringing on someone else's intellectual property or privacy;
  • false or intended to deceive.

5. Security and access

You must not:

  • try to gain unauthorised access to any part of the Platform, another account, or our systems;
  • interfere with or disrupt the Platform, for example by introducing malicious code or overloading it;
  • scrape, harvest or extract data at scale without our permission;
  • bypass or attempt to bypass any security or access controls.

6. Misuse of accounts

You must not share your account in a way that breaches these rules, impersonate another person, or create accounts to get around a suspension or restriction.

7. Reporting

If you see something on the Platform that breaks these rules, including a Player profile that should not be there, tell us using our contact form. We take reports seriously and will look into them.

8. What happens if you break these rules

If you breach this policy, we may remove content, suspend or close your account, and where necessary take further action or report the matter to the relevant authorities. We will act reasonably and in line with our Terms and Conditions.

9. Contact

For any question about this policy, contact us using our contact form.
